modules.name: fastimport.dates
documentation:
Date parsing routines.

Each routine represents a date format that can be specified in a
stream using the date-format feature.  The return value is
timestamp,timezone where

* timestamp is seconds since epoch
* timezone is the offset from UTC in seconds.
Copyright (C) 2008 Canonical Ltd

functions.name: fastimport.dates.parse_raw
documentation:
Parse a date from a raw string.

The format must be exactly "seconds-since-epoch offset-utc".
See the spec for details.

functions.name: fastimport.dates.parse_tz
documentation:
Parse a timezone specification in the [+|-]HHMM format.

```
return

the timezone offset in seconds.
```

functions.name: fastimport.dates.parse_rfc2822
documentation:
Parse a date from a rfc2822 string.

See the spec for details.

functions.name: fastimport.dates.parse_now
documentation:
Parse a date from a string.

The format must be exactly "now".
See the spec for details.

functions.name: fastimport.helpers.common_directory
documentation:
Find the deepest common directory of a list of paths.

```
return

if no paths are provided, None is returned;
if there is no common directory, '' is returned;
otherwise the common directory with a trailing / is returned.
```

functions.name: fastimport.helpers.is_inside
documentation:
True if fname is inside directory.

The parameters should typically be passed to osutils.normpath first, so
that . and .. and repeated slashes are eliminated, and the separators
are canonical for the platform.

The empty string as a dir name is taken as top-of-tree and matches
everything.

functions.name: fastimport.helpers.is_inside_any
documentation: True if fname is inside any of given dirs.

classes.name: fastimport.helpers.newobject

methods.name: fastimport.helpers.newobject.__long__
documentation: Are these ever needed?
def __div__(self):
return self.__truediv__()
def __idiv__(self, other):
return self.__itruediv__(other)

methods.name: fastimport.helpers.newobject.__native__
documentation: Hook for the future.utils.native() function
def __new__(cls, *args, **kwargs):
dict() -> new empty dictionary
dict(mapping) -> new dictionary initialized from a mapping object's
(key, value) pairs
dict(iterable) -> new dictionary initialized as if via:
for k, v in iterable:
d[k] = v
dict(**kwargs) -> new dictionary initialized with the name=value
pairs in the keyword argument list.
For example:  dict(one=1, two=2)
if len(args) == 0:
return super(newdict, cls).__new__(cls)
elif type(args[0]) == newdict:
return args[0]
else:
value = args[0]
return super(newdict, cls).__new__(cls, value)

A magical object class that provides Python 2 compatibility methods::

: next
  \_\_unicode\_\_
  \_\_nonzero\_\_

Subclasses of this class can merely define the Python 3 methods (\_\_next\_\_,
\_\_str\_\_, and \_\_bool\_\_).

This is a copy/paste of the future.types.newobject class of the future
package.

modules.name: fastimport.processor
documentation:
Processor for fast-import commands.

This module provides the skeleton of a fast-import backend.
To import from a fast-import stream to your version-control system:

 - derive a class from the abstract ImportProcessor class and
   implement the *_helper methods.

 - parse a fast-import stream into a sequence of commands, for example
   using the helpers from the parser module.

 - pass that command sequence to the process method of your processor.

See git-fast-import.1 for the meaning of each command and the
processors package for examples.
Copyright (C) 2008 Canonical Ltd
This program is free software; you can redistribute it and/or modify
it under the terms of the GNU General Public License as published by

modules.name: fastimport
documentation:
Fastimport file format parser and generator

This is a Python parser for git's fast-import format.  It was
originally developed for bzr-fastimport but has been extracted so
it can be used by other projects.  Use it like so:

   import fastimport.processor
   import fastimport.parser

   class ImportProcessor(fastimport.processor.ImportProcessor):
       ...

   parser = fastimport.parser.ImportParser(sys.stdin)
   processor = ImportProcessor(...)
   processor.process(parser.parse())

An import processor that dumps statistics about the input.

No changes to the current repository are made.

As well as providing useful information about an import
stream before importing it, this processor is useful for
benchmarking the speed at which data can be extracted from
the source.

methods.name: fastimport.processors.filter_processor.FilterProcessor._convert_rename
documentation:
Convert a FileRenameCommand into a new FileCommand.

```
return

None if the rename is being ignored, otherwise a
new FileCommand based on the whether the old and new paths
are inside or outside of the interesting locations.
```

methods.name: fastimport.processors.filter_processor.FilterProcessor._convert_copy
documentation:
Convert a FileCopyCommand into a new FileCommand.

```
return

None if the copy is being ignored, otherwise a
new FileCommand based on the whether the source and destination
paths are inside or outside of the interesting locations.
```

An import processor that filters the input to include/exclude objects.

No changes to the current repository are made.

Here are the supported parameters:

* include_paths - a list of paths that commits must change in order to
  be kept in the output stream

* exclude_paths - a list of paths that should not appear in the output
  stream

* squash_empty_commits - if set to False, squash commits that don't have
  any changes after the filter has been applied

methods.name: fastimport.parser.LineBasedParser.readline
documentation: Get the next line including the newline or '' on EOF.

methods.name: fastimport.parser.LineBasedParser.next_line
documentation: Get the next line without the newline or None on EOF.

methods.name: fastimport.parser.LineBasedParser.push_line
documentation:
Push line back onto the line buffer.

```
param line

the line with no trailing newline
```

methods.name: fastimport.parser.LineBasedParser.read_bytes
documentation:
Read a given number of bytes from the input stream.

Throws MissingBytes if the bytes are not found.

Note: This method does not read from the line buffer.

```
return

a string
```

methods.name: fastimport.parser.LineBasedParser.read_until
documentation:
Read the input stream until the terminator is found.

Throws MissingTerminator if the terminator is not found.

Note: This method does not read from the line buffer.

```
return

the bytes read up to but excluding the terminator.
```

methods.name: fastimport.parser.ImportParser.__init__
documentation:
A Parser of import commands.

```
param input_stream

the file-like object to read from

param verbose

display extra information of not

param output

the file-like object to write messages to (YAGNI?)

param user_mapper

if not None, the UserMapper used to adjust
user-ids for authors, committers and taggers.

param strict

Raise errors on strictly invalid data
```

methods.name: fastimport.parser.ImportParser.iter_commands
documentation: Iterator returning ImportCommand objects.

methods.name: fastimport.parser.ImportParser.iter_file_commands
documentation:
Iterator returning FileCommand objects.

If an invalid file command is found, the line is silently
pushed back and iteration ends.

methods.name: fastimport.parser.ImportParser._parse_blob
documentation: Parse a blob command.

methods.name: fastimport.parser.ImportParser._parse_commit
documentation: Parse a commit command.

methods.name: fastimport.parser.ImportParser._parse_feature
documentation: Parse a feature command.

methods.name: fastimport.parser.ImportParser._parse_file_modify
documentation:
Parse a filemodify command within a commit.

```
param info

a string in the format "mode dataref path"
(where dataref might be the hard-coded literal 'inline').
```

methods.name: fastimport.parser.ImportParser._parse_reset
documentation: Parse a reset command.

methods.name: fastimport.parser.ImportParser._parse_tag
documentation: Parse a tag command.

methods.name: fastimport.parser.ImportParser._get_mark_if_any
documentation: Parse a mark section.

methods.name: fastimport.parser.ImportParser._get_from
documentation: Parse a from section.

methods.name: fastimport.parser.ImportParser._get_merge
documentation: Parse a merge section.

methods.name: fastimport.parser.ImportParser._get_property
documentation: Parse a property section.

methods.name: fastimport.parser.ImportParser._get_user_info
documentation: Parse a user section.

methods.name: fastimport.parser.ImportParser._get_data
documentation: Parse a data section.

methods.name: fastimport.parser.ImportParser._who_when
documentation:
Parse who and when information from a string.

```
return

a tuple of (name,email,timestamp,timezone). name may be
the empty string if only an email address was given.
```

methods.name: fastimport.parser.ImportParser._name_value
documentation: Parse a (name,value) tuple from 'name value-length value'.

methods.name: fastimport.parser.ImportParser._path
documentation: Parse a path.

methods.name: fastimport.parser.ImportParser._path_pair
documentation: Parse two paths separated by a space.

methods.name: fastimport.parser.ImportParser._mode
documentation:
Check file mode format and parse into an int.

```
return

mode as integer
```
